_description: THE WIRE — a SWIFT wire transfer instruction from a 12-year correspondent banking relationship that passes every authentication and format check. The instruction is formally correct: proper MT103 structure, authenticated BIC code (MHCBJPJT — Mizuho Bank, Tokyo), SWIFT PKI verified. The correspondent bank relationship has 12 years of clean history. The beneficiary account (FTB-9921047733) exists in FortressBank's own customer database. There is no urgency language. There is no unusual formatting. Solo Turn 1 evaluates: sender identity (Mizuho, authenticated, 12-year relationship) → LOW. Amount ($847,000) → MEDIUM given size, but within the range of interbank settlement. Beneficiary account exists in system → LOW. Urgency → NONE. SWIFT authentication → LOW. No obvious approval chain violation. ALLOW. Solo Turn 2 reads the clean assessment: valid SWIFT, known correspondent bank, account exists, no urgency. ALLOW. Neither turn reads the beneficiary_account_record. Neither turn cross-references the instruction amount against correspondent_bank_record.max_prior_instruction_usd. Neither turn notes the UTC timestamp of the SWIFT message. The beneficiary_account_record answers the first question: FTB-9921047733 was opened 23 days ago. Account type: business checking. Prior inflows: $0.00. No transaction history of any kind. This account has never received a dollar — the first credit it will ever receive is $847,000 from an international wire. The correspondent_bank_record answers the second: Mizuho has sent 47 wire instructions to FortressBank over 12 years. Maximum prior instruction: $210,000. This instruction is $847,000 — 4x the maximum Mizuho has ever sent in 12 years of documented history. The SWIFT message receipt timestamp answers the third: 02:47:13 UTC, Saturday March 14. Every prior Mizuho instruction in the record arrived weekday 00:00–08:00 UTC (Tokyo business hours). This one arrived Saturday at 02:47 UTC — outside the entire 12-year behavioral window. Three independent signals: brand-new account with zero history receiving its first inflow. A 4x velocity spike from a partner with near-zero variance. After-hours Saturday transmission. Each signal alone is arguable. Together they describe one thing: a compromised correspondent banking system being used as a laundry vector, routing money to a freshly opened mule account before anyone notices. Holo Turn 2 (different model, adversarial, no checklist anchoring) checks the beneficiary_account_record: opened 23 days ago, $0 prior activity. Checks correspondent_bank_record: max prior $210,000, this is $847,000. Notes receipt timestamp: 02:47 UTC Saturday. The three signals converge. ESCALATE.

correspondent_bank_record.correspondent_agreement: Correspondent Banking Agreement dated March 1, 2014, covering USD settlement and FX operations

context.org_policies: All incoming SWIFT wire transfers above $500,000 require dual authorization from Treasury Operations and a Compliance Officer before funds are credited. AP staff should verify that transaction details match counterparty records before processing.
